articles.title: Odisha Declares Early School Summer Vacation Amid Heat Wave Concerns
articles.search_term: (heatwave OR "heat wave" OR "severe heatwave" OR "extreme heat" OR "heat spell" OR "scorching heat" OR "blistering heat" OR "sweltering heat" OR "hot winds" OR loo OR "hot loo winds") Odisha
articles.full_text:
Odisha Declares Early School Summer Vacation Amid Heat Wave Concerns
Odisha's Chief Minister Mohan Charan Majhi has announced early summer vacations for schools starting April 27 due to a prevailing heat wave. This decision follows recommendations from the School and Mass Education Department. Despite the vacation, examinations, census, and official activities will proceed as scheduled. OSEPA will conduct a Summer Learning Programme for students from Shishu Vatika to class 8 to ensure continued learning.
Odisha Chief Minister Mohan Charan Majhi declared an early summer vacation for schools starting April 27, citing current heat wave conditions.
The decision was made on the School and Mass Education Department's recommendation, though exams and official activities will continue as planned.
The Odisha School Education Programme Authority has initiated a Summer Learning Programme for students in government schools, ensuring learning continuity through assignments and audio-visual materials.
(With inputs from agencies.)

articles.title: Odisha CM Mohan Charan Majhi Announces Early Summer Vacation From April 27 Amid Heatwave
articles.search_term: (heatwave OR "heat wave" OR "severe heatwave" OR "extreme heat" OR "heat spell" OR "scorching heat" OR "blistering heat" OR "sweltering heat" OR "hot winds" OR loo OR "hot loo winds") Odisha
articles.full_text:
New Delhi: Odisha Chief Minister Mohan Charan Majhi on Friday announced early summer vacation in schools across the state due to the prevailing heatwave.
As per the order, all government, government-aided and private schools will remain closed from April 27, while pre-scheduled examinations and official work will continue as planned.
The Chief Minister has approved the related proposal from the School and Mass Education Department.
In a post on X, the state's Information and Public Relations Department wrote, "Chief Minister Shri Mohan Charan Majhi has announced an early summer vacation keeping in view the severe heatwave currently sweeping through the state. Keeping the safety of children in mind, the Chief Minister has taken this decision. The Chief Minister has approved the related proposal from the School and Mass Education Department. According to this decision, all government, government-aided, and private schools in the state will remain closed from April 27. However, previously scheduled examinations, census work, and other office tasks will continue as before."
Meanwhile, rising temperatures continue to impact several parts of the country. In a separate incident, a 37-year-old man undergoing treatment for sunstroke died on Thursday, highlighting the severity of the heat conditions.
The deceased has been identified as Sunil Kumar, a native of Pallippoyil in Kannur.
The incident occurred around 11 am on Wednesday while Sunil was engaged in digging a well at his house. He later collapsed and was rushed to a nearby clinic in Pallippoyil before being shifted to a private hospital. Though his condition initially showed signs of improvement, he passed away early in the morning.
The India Meteorological Department (IMD) has issued heatwave warnings for multiple regions, with temperatures in several states ranging between 40°C and 45°C. Similar conditions have been reported in parts of Odisha, Telangana, Madhya Pradesh, and Uttar Pradesh, among others.
According to the India Meteorological Department (IMD), maximum temperatures ranging between 40°C and 45°C were recorded over most parts of Vidarbha, many areas of Marathwada and Madhya Pradesh, and at several places in Chhattisgarh, Telangana and Uttar Pradesh.
Also Watch:
IMD said similar conditions were observed at isolated places over interior Odisha, Rayalaseema, Jharkhand, Rajasthan and Gujarat, while temperatures remained in the 36°C-40°C range over most other parts of the country, except parts of the western Himalayan region and Northeast India, where readings were below 36°C.
The weather agency added that maximum temperatures were markedly above normal in parts of Himachal Pradesh, Bihar, Uttarakhand and Chhattisgarh. They were appreciably above normal in several parts of Jharkhand, East Uttar Pradesh, Delhi-NCR, Punjab, Jammu and Kashmir, Madhya Pradesh and Kerala, among others.
IMD further stated that above-normal temperatures were recorded in parts of Rajasthan, Maharashtra, Karnataka and other regions, while near-normal conditions prevailed over Andaman and Nicobar Islands, Gujarat, coastal Karnataka and Lakshadweep.

articles.title: Odisha prepones summer vacation 2026: Schools to close from April 27, amid heatwave - India Today
articles.full_text:
India's Best Colleges 2025
ADVERTISEMENT
Odisha prepones summer vacation 2026: Schools to close from April 27, amid heatwave
The Odisha government has announced the preponement of summer vacation 2026 for schools amid rising heatwave conditions across the state. Students can check the complete details here.
Due to rising temperatures and students' safety concerns, Chief Minister Mohan Charan Majhi has announced early school closure across Odisha, as IMD warnings highlight extreme heatwave conditions affecting multiple districts.
Schools will remain closed from April 27, 2026, and this applies to government, aided, and private schools.
Odisha is experiencing an intense heatwave where the temperatures have crossed 40°C in several regions.
In recent days, multiple districts in Odisha have already ordered temporary school closures due to the heatwave. The statewide decision ensures a uniform holiday across all regions as temperatures are expected to rise further in the coming weeks.
"Chief Minister Shri Mohan Charan Majhi has announced an early summer vacation, keeping in view the severe heatwave. The Chief Minister has approved the related proposal from the School and Mass Education Department," the official tweet by the I & PR Department read. "According to this decision, all government, government-aided, and private schools in the state will remain closed from April 27. However, previously scheduled examinations, census work, and other office tasks will continue as before."
Keep visiting the official website for more such updates and notifications.

articles.title: Summer vacation: Odisha announces school holidays from April 27 due to heatwave; details inside - India.Com
articles.full_text:
Summer vacation: Odisha announces school holidays from April 27 due to heatwave; details inside
Summer vacation: The decision of announcing summer vacation in advance in Odisha has been taken due to the rising temperature and heatwave conditions. Scroll down for details.
School holidays in Odisha: In a major update from Odisha, the state has announced summer vacation in schools from April 27, i.e., Monday. The announcement was made by the chief minister, Mohan Charan Majhi, on Friday. The decision comes after the rising heatwave conditions in the state. The ministry came up with the decision after a recommendation from the School and Mass Education Department.
Odisha government announces school holidays
“All government, government-aided, and private schools in the state will close for summer vacations from April 27. However, previously scheduled examinations, census work, and other office tasks will continue as before,” stated the Government of Odisha.
All government, government-aided, and private schools in the state will close for summer vacations from April 27. However, previously scheduled examinations, census work, and other office tasks will continue as before: Government of Odisha
— ANI (@ANI) April 24, 2026
Other activities to continue as per the schedule
The announcement of school holidays was made on the recommendation of the school and mass education departments. The activities like examinations, census work, and other official work will continue to happen according to the schedule.
The school and mass education minister, Nityananda Gond, stated that summer vacations usually start from May 6 and end on June 17.
“However, considering the existing heat wave condition, summer vacation has been announced earlier,” he said, as reported by PTI.
Heatwave review
The decision on the early summer vacation in Odisha comes after the state government emphasised the consideration of starting the summer vacation in advance. This is because of the rising temperature and growing heatwave conditions in the state. The decision has been taken for the health of the children.
Weather in Odisha
The Indian Meteorological Department (IMD) earlier mentioned that hot and humid weather conditions are likely to prevail in the isolated regions over Konkan, Gangetic West Bengal, and Odisha from April 23 to 25.
मुख्य बिंदु
(i) अगले 3-4 दिनों के दौरान उत्तर-पश्चिम के मैदानी इलाकों में और मध्य भारत में उष्ण लहर चलने की संभावना है।
(ii) उप-हिमालयी पश्चिम बंगाल और सिक्किम में 25 और 26 अप्रैल को, गंगीय पश्चिम बंगाल और झारखंड में 26 से 28 अप्रैल के दौरान और बिहार में 26 और 27 अप्रैल को गरज… pic.twitter.com/qwY3w7Vjqf
— India Meteorological Department (@Indiametdept) April 24, 2026
The official notification by the weather department on X read, “Hot & humid weather conditions very likely to prevail in isolated pockets over Coastal Karnataka, Konkan & Goa, Saurashtra & Kutch, Gangetic West Bengal, Odisha and Bihar on 24th & 25th; Tripura during 24th -26th; Tamil Nadu, Puducherry & Karaikal during 24th -30th; Kerala & Mahe during 24th -26th and Coastal Andhra Pradesh & Yanam during 24th -28th April.”
(With inputs from ANI and PTI)

articles.title: Odisha CM Announces Early Summer Vacation For Schools In State Due To Severe Heatwave - ETV Bharat
articles.full_text:
Odisha CM Announces Early Summer Vacation For Schools In State Due To Severe Heatwave
All government, government-aided and private schools across the state will remain closed from April 27.
Bhubaneswar: Schools across Odisha will close from April 27 for summer vacations due to the prevailing heatwave.
Chief Minister Mohan Charan Majhi made the announcement on Friday.
As per an order, all government, government-aided and private schools will remain closed from April 27, while pre-scheduled examinations and official work will continue as planned. The Chief Minister has approved the related proposal from the School and Mass Education Department. However, the Chief Minister's office has informed that the pre-scheduled examinations, census work and other office work will continue as usual.
Similarly, children enrolled in anganwadis will be given dry food during the vacation. Arrangements have also been made to ensure that there is no interruption in classes during the summer vacation.
In a post on X, the state’s Information and Public Relations Department wrote, “Chief Minister Shri Mohan Charan Majhi has announced an early summer vacation keeping in view the severe heatwave currently sweeping through the state. Keeping the safety of children in mind, the Chief Minister has taken this decision.

articles.title: Odisha schools to start early summer vacation from April 27 due to heatwave - The News Mill
articles.full_text:
Odisha Chief Minister Mohan Charan Majhi has announced an early summer vacation for schools across the state, effective from April 27, in response to the ongoing severe heatwave.
The decision applies to all government, government-aided, and private schools, which will remain closed from the specified date. However, pre-scheduled examinations, census work, and official duties will continue as planned. This proposal was approved by the Chief Minister following recommendations from the School and Mass Education Department.
The state’s Information and Public Relations Department stated on X: “Chief Minister Shri Mohan Charan Majhi has announced an early summer vacation keeping in view the severe heatwave currently sweeping through the state. Keeping the safety of children in mind, the Chief Minister has taken this decision. The Chief Minister has approved the related proposal from the School and Mass Education Department. According to this decision, all government, government-aided, and private schools in the state will remain closed from April 27. However, previously scheduled examinations, census work, and other office tasks will continue as before.”
Meanwhile, the impact of rising temperatures continues to affect several parts of India. A 37-year-old man receiving treatment for sunstroke died on Thursday, highlighting the severity of the heat conditions. The deceased, Sunil Kumar, was from Pallippoyil in Kannur.
The incident occurred around 11 am on Wednesday while Kumar was digging a well at his home. He collapsed and was taken to a nearby clinic in Pallippoyil before being transferred to a private hospital. Although his condition initially improved, he died early on Thursday morning.
The India Meteorological Department (IMD) has issued heatwave warnings for multiple regions, with temperatures ranging between 40°C and 45°C recorded in several states including Vidarbha, Marathwada, Madhya Pradesh, Chhattisgarh, Telangana, and Uttar Pradesh.
Similar high temperature conditions were observed at isolated locations in interior Odisha, Rayalaseema, Jharkhand, Rajasthan, and Gujarat. Most other parts of the country experienced temperatures between 36°C and 40°C, except for the western Himalayan region and Northeast India where readings were below 36°C.
The IMD noted that maximum temperatures were markedly above normal in parts of Himachal Pradesh, Bihar, Uttarakhand, and Chhattisgarh; appreciably above normal in several areas of Jharkhand, East Uttar Pradesh, Delhi-NCR, Punjab, Jammu and Kashmir, Madhya Pradesh, and Kerala; and above normal in parts of Rajasthan, Maharashtra, Karnataka, and other regions. Near-normal temperatures prevailed over the Andaman and Nicobar Islands, Gujarat, coastal Karnataka, and Lakshadweep.
